events.label: reasoning
events.text: The scenario describes a harness impact indicator that has been deployed (webbing tag visible). This means the harness has been subjected to an impact load and must be removed from service. This is a failed/defective condition.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "critical",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "failed", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 80,

  "findings": [
    "Full-body harness impact indicator (webbing tag) is deployed, indicating the harness has absorbed an impact load",
    "Harness subjected to fall arrest or impact must be permanently removed from service",
    "Deployed impact indicator is a visible defect cue confirming energy-absorbing system activation"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Immediately remove the harness from service and tag it as damaged/retired",
    "Inspect all associated components (lanyard, SRL, anchors, connectors) for impact damage",
    "Replace harness with a new, inspected unit before any further work at height resumes",
    "Document the event and investigate the cause of the deployed indicator"
  ],
  "rationale": "A deployed impact indicator on a full-body harness confirms the harness has experienced an arrest-level load. OSHA requires personal fall arrest system components subjected to impact to be removed from service and not used again until determined safe by a competent person or replaced. A harness with a deployed indicator cannot be recertified for use and must be retired.",
  "references": [
    "OSHA 1926.502(d)(19) - personal fall arrest system inspection and removal from service after impact",
    "OSHA 1910.140(c)(18) - personal fall protection system components subjected to impact must be removed from service",
    "OSHA 1910.140(d) - inspection requirements for personal fall protection systems"
  ]
}
